source_paper_title: Exactness and Effective Degree Bound of Lasserre’s Relaxation for Polynomial Optimization over Finite Variety
context:
Section 8 (Conclusion and perspectives), page 21 (PDF page 21/29)

However, Assumption 1 is stronger than the regular sequence property of \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\), see Section C. Indeed, \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is still a regular sequence if \(\{g^\infty_1,\dots,g^\infty_n\}\) has finitely many complex common zero locus. However, the H-basis property may fail if we only assume that \(\{\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\}\) is a regular sequence. It would be interesting to investigate the degree bound under the regular sequence condition only.
title: Effective degree bounds for exactness of Lasserre relaxations under the regular sequence condition (without Assumption 1)
background_latex:
Let \(n\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(g_1,\dots,g_n\in\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]\) be polynomials with degrees \(\deg(g_i)\ge 1\). For each \(i\), let \(\bar g_i\in\mathbb{R}[x_0,x_1,\dots,x_n]\) denote the homogenization of \(g_i\): \(\bar g_i(x_0,\dots,x_n)=x_0^{\deg(g_i)}g_i(x_1/x_0,\dots,x_n/x_0)\). Let \(S:=\mathbb{R}[x_0,\dots,x_n]\) and let \(I:=\langle \bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\rangle\subseteq S\) be the homogeneous ideal they generate.
A sequence \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\) is called a regular sequence in \(S\) if \(\langle \bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\rangle\neq S\) and for each \(i=1,\dots,n\), \(\bar g_i\) is not a zero divisor in \(S/\langle \bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_{i-1}\rangle\).
Consider a polynomial optimization problem of the form
\[\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} f(x)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad g_1(x)=\cdots=g_n(x)=0,\; h_1(x)\ge 0,\dots,h_m(x)\ge 0,\]
where \(f,h_1,\dots,h_m\in\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]\). Let \(f^*\) be the optimal value. Let \(\Sigma[x_1,\dots,x_n]\) be the cone of sum-of-squares (SOS) polynomials, let \(\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)\) be the quadratic module generated by \(1,h_1,\dots,h_m\), and let \(\langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle\) be the ideal generated by the equality constraints (with \(k\ge n\) allowed). For \(d\in\mathbb{N}\), let \(\langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle_{2d}\) and \(\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)_{2d}\) denote the standard degree-\(2d\) truncations used in Lasserre’s moment/SOS hierarchy.
problem_definition_latex:
Under the assumption that the homogenized polynomials \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\in S\) form a regular sequence (but without assuming Assumption 1, i.e., without requiring that the highest-degree parts \(g_1^\infty,\dots,g_n^\infty\) have no common nontrivial complex zero), derive an explicit effective bound \(d\) (in terms of \(\deg(f),\deg(g_i),\deg(h_j)\) and possibly other explicitly computable invariants implied by regularity) such that the truncated exactness certificate
\[f-f^*\in \langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle_{2d}+\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)_{2d}\]
holds whenever the (untruncated) membership \(f-f^*\in \langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle+\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)\) holds (equivalently, when Lasserre’s hierarchy is exact at some order).
